methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.0B - $1.6B.

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
AbCellera, a public-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, is evaluated against a comp universe of 3 same-sector peers at public stage: Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.6B), Freenome ($1.1B), and Alto Neuroscience ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across these three comps is $1.1B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.6B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because AbCellera's revenue is not on file; the broader Biotech & Health sector median P/S of 6.9x across tickers RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L is not actionable without the target's top-line data.

The single-method triangulation yields a median valuation of $1.1B, supported by a range of $935M to $1.3B. Key uncertainties include AbCellera's actual revenue and earnings trajectory post-IPO, which would enable revenue multiple validation, and the stability of peer valuations given volatility in public biotech. The peer set is constrained to three comps; expansion to the identified private peer universe (Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and others) would require harmonizing stage definitions. Until material financial disclosures emerge, the $1.1B median represents the best-supported estimate within the $935M–$1.3B band.
